title: Marie; a story of Russian love
summaries: "Marie; a story of Russian love" by Aleksandr Sergeevich Pushkin is a historical novel first published in 1836. Set during Pugachev's Rebellion of 1773-1774, it follows young officer Pyotr Grinyov as he navigates love, loyalty, and survival at a remote fortress. When the rebel leader Pugachev seizes control, Pyotr must balance his duty to the Empress with an unexpected bond formed during a blizzard. His love for the captain's daughter Masha becomes entangled with accusations of treason, forcing her to seek justice from Catherine the Great herself. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
